segments.text: People suddenly started seeing road cabs everywhere in London. You see, Sephora bought road for a billion dollars. And these all cabs, they're a part of their
plan together. You see, they look like regular caps, but they were actually rolling demo cars. You step inside and the experience begins. Free samples,
perfect mirrors, and everything is designed to make you try the product.
But why the cabs only? A commute is dead time. It's boring. Road did not just interrupt the customer day. They improved their dead time. A long, boring
ride transformed into a fun, patient 10-minute trial. Customer did not just see the picture of the product. They tested it. They felt it. And they finally fell in love with the product.
Win for both. Also, everyone who used the cap got a bonus. They can exchange their receipt for a free product at Sephora. You see, that's a crucial step
because it forces the first instore visit. Steals the first conversion and the first trial become initial loyalty.
From there, the product quality takes over. buy again and they buy again and in no time their entire cosmetic portfolio is rude and that is some genius [ __ ]
